items.title: China sanctions US tech giants weeks after Pentagon blacklisted Chinese firms

The companies barred from doing business with the US include drone makers and some involved in rare earths. Beijing's move comes weeks after the Pentagon slapped similar restrictions on Chinese companies.

items.title: Energy crisis: Time for the EU's Independence Day?

Soaring prices are threatening growth and fuelling inflation. Against this backdrop, investments in renewable energy are no longer about meeting climate targets. They are existential, and central to achieving competitiveness and strategic independence for Europe.

items.title: Trump-backed de la Espriella wins razor-tight Colombian presidential election

Cepeda told supporters he would challenge results from more than 30,000 voting stations.

items.title: Ukrainian attacks prompt Russian-held Crimea to halt civilian gasoline sales
items.description: Governor Sergey Aksyonov, the Kremlin-appointed head of Crimea, said that overnight Ukrainian strikes killed four people and wounded 28 others. He did not specify the target of the attack.

items.title: New Russian strikes hit Kharkiv and Odesa, killing one and injuring children
items.description: New Russian strikes hit Ukraine's Kharkiv and Odesa regions on 19 June, killing one person, injuring children and adults, and damaging homes, vehicles and fuel facilities overnight.

items.title: Australia confirms first case of H5N1 bird flu as virus reaches every continent
items.description: Australia was previously the only continent where the H5N1 bird flu strain had not yet been found.

items.title: Morocco captain Achraf Hakimi to face rape trial, French court rules

items.title: Lebanon air raids kill 18 as residents flee southern villages

items.title: Starmer resignation result of 'geopolitics' and shifting mood for 'flash and sparkle'
items.description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer resigned on June 22, paving the way for Britain to have its seventh prime minister in just over a decade. Andy Burnham, who won a special parliamentary election a few days earlier, is the front-runner to replace him as Labour leader. FRANCE 24's Douglas Herbert tells us more about what led Starmer to his resignation.
